title: REGIME_PROFILE — United Kingdom regulatory regime for university IT
jurisdiction: United Kingdom (GB; Northern Ireland variations noted where relevant)
jurisdictionType: post-Brexit; retained-EU-law derived, now diverging via domestic reform
method: Research over REAL primary UK regulation and authoritative secondary sources (legislation.gov.uk, ICO, NCSC, GOV.UK/GDS, Jisc). No SUIT prior document was used as a source. Each functional obligation F1-F6 is mapped to its concrete UK instrument(s), competent authority, and deadline or transfer mechanism.
note: The UK is no longer in the EU regime: GDPR/NIS/eIDAS survive as UK-retained-and-amended instruments rather than directly applicable EU law, and accessibility/equality run on domestic statutes. The minimal functional set is invariant; only its UK instantiation differs from the EU baseline.

competentAuthorities.dataProtection: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO), transitioning to the Information Commission under the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (new powers from 5 February 2026)
competentAuthorities.networkInformationSecurity: Sector-specific competent authorities for operators of essential services (NIS Regs Schedule 1); ICO for relevant digital service providers; NCSC as single point of contact and national CSIRT / technical authority
competentAuthorities.trustServices: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) as UK eIDAS supervisory body
competentAuthorities.digitalAccessibility: Government Digital Service (GDS) — monitoring; Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) — enforcement (Equality Commission for Northern Ireland in NI)

functions.name: Data protection
functions.instrument: UK GDPR (retained Regulation (EU) 2016/679 as amended for the UK) read with the Data Protection Act 2018 (c.12), amended by the Data (Use and Access) Act 2025 (c.18)
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Personal-data breach notification to the ICO without undue delay and, where feasible, within 72 hours of becoming aware (UK GDPR Art. 33); affected individuals notified where high risk (Art. 34)
functions.note: Domestic successor to the EU GDPR; DPA 2018 supplies exemptions (incl. education and research) and the Commissioner's powers. DUAA 2025 restructures the ICO into the Information Commission and makes targeted UK GDPR amendments (key provisions in force 5 February 2026).

functions.name: Security of processing
functions.instrument: UK GDPR Article 32 (security of processing) with the Data Protection Act 2018; ICO accountability and security guidance
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Standing obligation: appropriate technical and organisational measures (encryption, access control, resilience, regular testing) proportionate to risk; enforced via ICO assessment/penalties up to GBP 17.5m or 4% of global annual turnover
functions.note: F2 is the security-of-processing limb of the same UK GDPR/DPA 2018 instrument as F1; no separate deadline, a continuing duty. For universities: student/staff/research data security.

functions.name: Network and information security (cyber resilience)
functions.instrument: The Network and Information Systems Regulations 2018 (SI 2018/506); forthcoming Cyber Security and Resilience (Network and Information Systems) Bill (introduced Nov 2025) as successor
functions.authority: Sector competent authorities (OES); ICO (relevant digital service providers); NCSC as single point of contact / national CSIRT and technical authority
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Significant/substantial incident notified to the relevant competent authority without undue delay and, where feasible, no later than 72 hours after becoming aware (NIS Regs reg. 11)
functions.note: UK transposition of the EU NIS Directive 2016/1148, in force 10 May 2018. Universities most often engage as relevant digital service providers (cloud/research-computing) under ICO, with NCSC guidance (Cyber Assessment Framework). Successor CSR Bill expands scope (e.g. managed service providers); not yet in force.

functions.name: International / cross-border data transfers
functions.instrument: UK GDPR Chapter V (Arts 44-49); ICO International Data Transfer Agreement (IDTA) and the UK Addendum to the EU SCCs (in force 21 March 2022); UK adequacy decisions; DUAA 2025 data-protection-test
functions.authority: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO); adequacy designations by the Secretary of State
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Transfer mechanism (no deadline): UK adequacy decision; otherwise IDTA or EU SCCs + UK Addendum plus a transfer risk assessment reflecting Schrems II. DUAA 2025 replaces essential-equivalence with a not-materially-lower data-protection-test
functions.note: F4 is a transfer-mechanism obligation, not a deadline obligation. For universities: lawful route to send personal data to non-UK cloud providers/collaborators.

functions.name: Electronic identification and trust services
functions.instrument: UK eIDAS — retained Regulation (EU) No 910/2014 as amended by the Electronic Identification and Trust Services (Amendment etc.) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019, with the Electronic Identification and Trust Services Regulations 2016 (SI 2016/696)
functions.authority: Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) as UK eIDAS supervisory body
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Conformity/qualified-status mechanism: ICO grants/revokes qualified status for UK trust service providers, with security-breach reporting, audits and enforcement; qualified e-signatures/seals carry legal effect
functions.note: Governs e-signatures, seals, time stamps, registered delivery and website-authentication certificates. For universities: qualified signing/sealing of diplomas, contracts, secure transactions.

functions.name: Digital accessibility
functions.instrument: The Public Sector Bodies (Websites and Mobile Applications) (No. 2) Accessibility Regulations 2018 (SI 2018/952); conformance to EN 301 549 / WCAG 2.1 AA (monitoring extended to WCAG 2.2 from October 2024)
functions.authority: Government Digital Service (GDS) — monitoring; Equality and Human Rights Commission (EHRC) — enforcement (ECNI in Northern Ireland)
functions.deadlineOrMechanism: Standing conformance obligation to WCAG 2.1 AA plus a published, accurate accessibility statement; GDS samples sites/statements and EHRC may investigate, issue unlawful-act notices and bring proceedings
functions.note: Most HE/FE providers are in scope through public funding. Jisc provides the authoritative HE-sector reading. Underlying equality duties also flow from the Equality Act 2010 (anticipatory reasonable-adjustment duty).
